title: Redis key prefix inventory: 27 unique prefixes identified
evidence: Prefixes found in source (non-test) .ts files: lock: (distributed-lock.ts default), lock:execution: (engine.ts), coordinator:leader:lock (coordinator.ts, cross-region-health.ts), bridge:recovery: (execution-engine/types.ts), region:health: (cross-region-health.ts), pair: (hierarchical-cache.ts, pair-cache.ts), price: (price-oracle.ts, price-matrix keys), ratelimit: (security), risk:probabilities: (execution-probability-tracker.ts, risk-config.ts), health:system:snapshot (enhanced-health-monitor.ts), health:<serviceName> (self-healing-manager.ts), health:circuit-breakers (integration test only), metrics:system:recent (enhanced-health-monitor.ts), quality:score:current (professional-quality-monitor.ts), quality:detection: (professional-quality-monitor.ts), opp:dedup: (publishing-service.ts), dlq: (dead-letter-queue.ts), trade: (performance-analytics.ts), performance_report: (performance-analytics.ts), service-config: (graceful-degradation.ts), routing:failed: (cross-region-health.ts), recovery_action: (expert-self-healing-manager.ts), health_state: (expert-self-healing-manager.ts), auth: (security integration tests), service-degradation: (documented in CLAUDE.md, not found in source), arbitrage: (event types only, not Redis keys), commit-reveal: (documented in CLAUDE.md, not found in source grep)
expected: All prefixes documented and no collisions
actual: 27 unique prefixes identified, no cross-service collisions detected. 2 prefixes documented in CLAUDE.md (service-degradation:, commit-reveal:) were not found in current source code grep -- may be behind feature flags or removed.
recommendation: No action needed. Maintain prefix registry in documentation as new keys are added.

title: Substring overlap: lock: is a prefix of lock:execution:
evidence: shared/core/src/redis/distributed-lock.ts uses default keyPrefix 'lock:' (line 162). services/execution-engine/src/engine.ts uses keyPrefix 'lock:execution:' (line 461). Both use the distributed-lock module which prepends the prefix to resource names.
expected: No substring overlaps that could cause accidental key collision
actual: lock: is a substring prefix of lock:execution:. However, the actual keys formed are lock:<resourceName> and lock:execution:<chain>, so they will only collide if a resource named 'execution:<chain>' is used with the default prefix. This is unlikely but theoretically possible.
recommendation: Document the prefix hierarchy. The risk is minimal since execution-engine explicitly sets its own prefix.

title: Substring overlap: health: prefix used by multiple subsystems
evidence: health:<serviceName> written by self-healing-manager.ts:734, health:system:snapshot by enhanced-health-monitor.ts:544, health:circuit-breakers in integration tests, health_state:<serviceName> by expert-self-healing-manager.ts:405. The SA-1O-002 fix previously moved system snapshot from health:snapshot to health:system:snapshot to avoid collision.
actual: health: prefix is shared across self-healing-manager, enhanced-health-monitor, and integration tests. The SA-1O-002 fix already addressed the worst collision. Remaining usage is consistent: health:<serviceName> for per-service state, health:system:snapshot for aggregate snapshot, health_state:<serviceName> for expert self-healing state.
recommendation: Consider consolidating health_state: into health: namespace for consistency, or document the distinction. Low risk since writers are well-separated.

title: ADR-022: No spread operators in loops in hot-path files
evidence: Searched for spread operators inside for/while/forEach/map/reduce/filter loops in: shared/core/src/caching/price-matrix.ts, shared/core/src/partitioned-detector.ts (not found -- may be in different path), services/execution-engine/src/*.ts, services/unified-detector/src/*.ts. No matches found. price-matrix.ts has 2 spread uses (lines 507, 1065) but both are in getter methods returning copies of config/stats objects, not in loops.
expected: No spread operators in loops on hot path

title: ADR-033: STALE_PRICE_THRESHOLD constant not found in price-matrix.ts
evidence: Searched shared/core/src/caching/price-matrix.ts for STALE_PRICE_THRESHOLD, 30000, 30_000. No matches. The getPriceWithFreshnessCheck() method at line 717 uses a configurable maxAgeMs parameter with default 5000ms. Gas-price-cache.ts has staleThresholdMs defaulting to 180000ms (3 min). No file in shared/core/src defines a STALE_PRICE_THRESHOLD of 30000ms.
expected: ADR-033 stale price window of 30000ms enforced in price-matrix.ts
actual: price-matrix.ts uses a per-call configurable maxAgeMs defaulting to 5000ms, not a global 30000ms constant. The ADR-033 threshold may be enforced at a higher layer or the ADR may reference a different constant name.
recommendation: Verify ADR-033 requirements. If 30s stale window is required, add STALE_PRICE_THRESHOLD = 30000 as a named constant and use it as the default for getPriceWithFreshnessCheck(). The current 5s default is stricter than 30s, so this may be intentional.

title: ADR-002: No direct inter-service HTTP calls found in service source code
evidence: Grepped for http://localhost:300[0-6] in services/**/*.ts. Found 4 matches, all in coordinator: 2 in test file (middleware.test.ts:218,248), 1 in middleware/index.ts:86 (CORS allowed origins list for dashboard UI). No service-to-service HTTP calls detected -- all inter-service communication uses Redis Streams.
expected: All inter-service communication via Redis Streams
actual: Compliant. The only http://localhost references are CORS origin allowlists for the coordinator dashboard, not inter-service calls.

title: ADR-018: Circuit breaker failureThreshold defaults consistently set to 5
evidence: shared/core/src/resilience/circuit-breaker.ts:408 default=5, shared/core/src/resilience/self-healing-manager.ts:36 default=5, services/execution-engine/src/types.ts:683 default=5, services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:99 default=5, services/execution-engine/src/strategies/uniswapx-filler.strategy.ts:188 explicit=5, shared/core/src/mev-protection/backrun-bundle-builder.ts:183 explicit=5, services/coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:47 default=5. The only non-5 value is mev-share-event-listener.ts:233 which dynamically sets failureThreshold=maxReconnectAttempts (documented as intentional).
actual: Compliant. All defaults are 5. Dynamic override in mev-share-event-listener is documented.

title: ADR-005: SharedArrayBuffer confirmed as primary price storage
evidence: shared/core/src/caching/price-matrix.ts uses SharedArrayBuffer for price and timestamp storage (lines 223-224, 438-439). Falls back to ArrayBuffer only when SharedArrayBuffer is unavailable (line 454). Worker thread access via getSharedBuffer() (line 1018).
expected: SharedArrayBuffer used for L1 cache price storage
actual: Compliant. SharedArrayBuffer is the primary storage mechanism with ArrayBuffer fallback.

title: Execution-engine Dockerfile EXPOSE and HEALTHCHECK use port 8080, conflicts with Fly.io/Docker Compose port 3005
evidence: services/execution-engine/Dockerfile line 17: EXPOSE 8080, line 19-20: HEALTHCHECK probes http://localhost:8080/health. But: Fly.io toml sets HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3005 and internal_port=3005 (execution-engine.toml:37,42). Docker Compose partition.yml sets HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3001 internally (line 385). Docker Compose base (docker-compose.yml) sets HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3005 (line 74). The EE code (index.ts:50) defaults to port 3005 via HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var. service-ports.json comment acknowledges this: 'execution-engine uses port 8080 in Fly.io production (overridden via HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var)'.
expected: Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK port matches the port the application actually listens on
actual: Dockerfile hardcodes port 8080 for EXPOSE and HEALTHCHECK, but the app listens on 3005 (default) or whatever HEALTH_CHECK_PORT is set to. When using Fly.io or Docker Compose, the env var override makes it work, but standalone Dockerfile usage will have a broken healthcheck. The service-ports.json comment is now stale since the Fly.io config was fixed to use 3005.
recommendation: Update services/execution-engine/Dockerfile: change EXPOSE 8080 to EXPOSE 3005, update HEALTHCHECK to probe port 3005. The service-ports.json comment about 8080 override is now stale and should be removed.

title: Execution-engine Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK interval 30s vs 15s standard
evidence: services/execution-engine/Dockerfile:19 HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s. All other Dockerfiles, Fly.io TOMLs, and Docker Compose files use 15s interval per ADR-007 standardization. Coordinator Dockerfile uses --interval=15s.
actual: Execution-engine Dockerfile uses 30s interval, diverging from the 15s standard.
recommendation: Update execution-engine Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK to --interval=15s for consistency.

title: All Fly.io TOML ports aligned with service-ports.json
evidence: coordinator.toml: port 3000 (matches service-ports.json coordinator: 3000). execution-engine.toml: port 3005 (matches). cross-chain-detector.toml: port 3006 (matches). partition-asia-fast.toml: port 3001 (matches). partition-l2-turbo.toml: port 3002 (matches). partition-high-value.toml: port 3003 (matches). partition-solana.toml: port 3004 (matches).
actual: All Fly.io TOML configs have internal_port, health check port, and HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var aligned with service-ports.json.

title: Health check paths consistent across all infrastructure configs
evidence: Coordinator: /api/health in Fly.io TOML, Docker Compose (all 3 files), and Dockerfile. All other services: /health in Fly.io TOMLs and Docker Compose files. Coordinator correctly uses /api prefix (Express router). No mismatches detected.
actual: Compliant. All health check paths match between Fly.io TOMLs, Docker Compose files, and Dockerfiles.

title: All Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine, aligned with engines requirement
evidence: All 19 Dockerfile FROM statements use node:22-alpine. Package.json requires engines >=22.0.0.

title: docker-compose.partition.yml Redis maxmemory comment says 256mb but actual config is 512mb
evidence: infrastructure/docker/docker-compose.partition.yml line 87 comment: 'docker-compose.partition.yml (prod): 256mb'. Line 98 actual command: '--maxmemory 512mb'. The comment is stale -- maxmemory was likely bumped from 256mb to 512mb but the comment table was not updated.
actual: Comment says 256mb, actual is 512mb. All three compose files now use 512mb uniformly.
recommendation: Update the comment block at lines 85-91 to reflect the current 512mb setting for docker-compose.partition.yml.

title: No timeout-related files changed since last run
evidence: Changed files: services/execution-engine/__tests__/unit/strategies/simulation.strategy.test.ts, services/execution-engine/src/strategies/simulation.strategy.ts. These are simulation strategy files, not timeout configuration.
expected: Skip incremental check for unrelated changes
actual: Skipped. No timeout-related files in the changeset.
